title: Exoskeleton
content:
{{Top|leg|notoc=1}}
An '''exoskeleton''' was an external [[Skeleton/Legends|skeleton]] in place of internal [[Bone/Legends|bones]] and was an anatomical feature of many [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] and non-sentient [[Species/Legends|species]], particularly [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] species. Exoskeletons were usually composed of [[Chitin/Legends|chitin]] and could be quite durable. Notable species that possessed exoskeletons included the [[Gand/Legends|Gand]], the [[Geonosian/Legends|Geonosians]], the [[Givin/Legends|Givin]], the [[Kamarian]]s, and the [[Verpine/Legends|Verpine]].

==Armor==
[[File:CloneBlazeTrooper.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Blaze trooper exoskeletons]]
An '''exoskeleton''' was also a large suit of [[Armor/Legends|armor]] designed to augment its wearer's strength. Often it had weapons built into it.

The [[Deck Master (New Hope)|Deck Master]] of the ''[[New Hope]]'' was wearing an exoskeleton suit while working on the ship's [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] bay.

Exoskeletons included some of the [[Dark Trooper/Legends|dark trooper]] variants and the [[light exoskeleton]] and [[heavy exoskeleton]]. Other examples included [[clone blaze trooper]] armor and the later [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Zero-G assault stormtrooper|spacetrooper]] armor.

During the [[Empire Reborn]] crisis, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Galak Fyyar]] made use of an exoskeleton-like [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] armor with which he [[Battle of Yavin 4 (Empire Reborn)|confronted]] [[Kyle Katarn]].
